finding_results.notes: The seasonal anchor now keeps the chapter in late November moving into early winter, which stays consistent with the November continuation in chapter 8.

The key now comes from an Abraxas gloss hidden in the ledger hinge, surfaced only after Hermione identifies the family mark and safely loosens the binding. Draco states why he had not already been using it, and both of them frame the gloss as suspect until it is tested against text they already understand.

finding_results.notes: The aftermath now adds a new suspicion about what Narcissa withheld and turns that into a concrete choice: Hermione conceals the warning from Draco. The ending no longer paraphrases the revelation again; it converts the resonance into a deliberate, ethically compromised record.
The final beat is Hermione beginning a new notebook record, not another sleep/stillness refrain.
Chapter-wide cleanup removed em-dash lines entirely.
Chapter-wide sentence restructuring reduced She-opened sentences to 9, below the threshold.
Chapter-wide diction cleanup removed all precise/precision/precisely references.
Draco's Dark Mark is now consistently placed on his left forearm.

summary: All eight packet findings were addressed. Chapter 7 now stays in late November, earns the Abraxas key through on-page discovery and verification, turns Narcissa's warning into Hermione's concealment and new record-keeping behavior, ends on forward motion instead of insomnia, and clears the dash, She-open, precision, and Dark Mark continuity checks.
